chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Divorce of a Woman Who Has Not Been Consummated and the Ruling on a Woman Whose Husband Passes Away Before or After Consummation

text: 4781 - وَ سَأَلَ شِهَابٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ بِامْرَأَةٍ بِأَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ فَأَدَّاهَا إِلَيْهَا فَوَهَبَتْهَا لَهُ وَ قَالَتْ أَنَا فِيكَ أَرْغَبُ فَطَلَّقَهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا قَالَ "يَرْجِعُ عَلَيْهَا بِخَمْسِمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4781 - Shihab asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who married a woman with a dowry of one thousand dirhams. He paid her the dowry, and she gifted it back to him, saying, "I desire you more."
However, he divorced her before consummating the marriage.
Imam (as) replied: "He is entitled to reclaim five hundred dirhams from her."
